done_summary:
Triaged all 30 support tickets from `/input/repo/tickets.txt`. Produced:

**`/workspace/triage_results.json`** — Structured JSON with:
- All 30 tickets classified into 12 categories (bug-report: 7, billing-refund: 4, spam-or-noise: 4, feature-request: 3, incident-active: 2, account-management: 2, auth-permissions: 2, external-business: 2, general-question: 2, legal-compliance: 1, security-incident: 1, billing-other: 0)
- Urgency levels: 6 urgent, 14 normal, 6 low, 4 n/a
- 2 duplicate clusters identified: [007, 011] (dashboard sharing permissions) and [014, 023, 030] (API rate limits)
- Detailed notes explaining non-obvious classification decisions

**`/workspace/README.md`** — Human-readable summary with urgency tables, recommended action order, and methodology

**`/workspace/decisions/adr-001-through-005.md`** — Architecture Decision Records for 5 non-obvious calls (rate limits classification, extortion email, duplicate detection, incident vs bug distinction, downtime credit)

Key urgent items requiring immediate attention:
1. Ticket 028 — Active security incident (account compromise)
2. Ticket 003 — Production outage (entire org locked out)
3. Ticket 016 — API 500 errors (SOC1 review deadline)
4. Tickets 014/023/030 — Rate limits blocking $48k customer
